title: Map UI config keys to canonical backend schema
description: Implement single API-boundary mapping from ui_v2_exp config naming to canonical planner schema and persist settings_used.json.
notes: Implemented canonical config mapping at API boundary in ui_v2_exp. Added ApiService.toCanonicalPriorityConfig() to normalize UI-oriented config (deadlines array, penaltySettings, proximityRules) into canonical backend schema and also normalize already-canonical inputs. Solver execute now always posts canonical priority_config and mirrors it as settings_used. Solver results path now injects settings_used.json from canonical config when backend response lacks it, ensuring traceability in output files. Added test suite ui_v2_exp/assets/js/services/apiService.test.js covering UI-shape mapping and execute payload canonicalization/settings_used behavior. Verification: npx jest ui_v2_exp/assets/js/services/apiService.test.js ui_v2_exp/assets/js/stores/configStore.test.js => 2 passed, 23 tests passed.
close_reason: Resolved by implementing API-boundary canonical config mapping and settings_used trace artifact behavior with passing tests.

title: ui_v2_exp visualizationStore uses new Function for template execution
description:
UBS static analysis flagged a critical security issue in ui_v2_exp/assets/js/stores/visualizationStore.js:218 where dynamic code is executed via new Function('d3', 'data', 'container', this.code). This creates a potential code-injection surface if template/code input becomes user-controlled or tampered.

Evidence:
- UBS v5.0.7 scan command: ubs ui_v2_exp
- Finding location: ui_v2_exp/assets/js/stores/visualizationStore.js:218

Expected:
- Visualization execution should avoid runtime eval/new Function paths for untrusted or mutable input.
- Use a safe allowlisted template registry or sandboxed execution model.

Actual:
- Code compiles and executes arbitrary JS source in the page context using new Function.

Suggested fix scope:
1) Replace new Function execution with template IDs mapped to vetted implementations.
2) If dynamic execution is unavoidable, enforce strict validation and isolate execution context.
3) Add regression tests proving only approved template logic can run.

Verification notes:
- UBS no longer reports the new Function security rule for visualizationStore.js after fix.
- Existing visualization functionality remains intact for all supported templates.
close_reason: completed

title: Produce plot.html and plot.png per run
description: Ensure each run/scenario writes visualization artifacts in plots/ with diagnostics for infeasible/failed runs.
close_reason: Completed: per-run plot.html and plot.png artifacts for success/failure statuses in planner_v4

title: HTMX fragments resolve to app shell, causing nested UI
description: When serving ui_v2_exp with SPA fallback, fragment requests such as /components/tabs.html are redirected and resolve to index shell content. HTMX inserts that full shell into #tabs-container, producing UI-inside-UI repetition. Repro: start with serve -s on port 4174, open /index.html, inspect fragment URL behavior for /components/tabs.html and /components/tabs. Expected: tabs fragment HTML only. Actual: index shell returned. Fix: ensure components/*.html bypasses SPA fallback or use non-SPA static server for this app. Verify by confirming /components/tabs.html returns fragment markup and page renders single shell.
notes: Implemented fix in ui_v2_exp/serve.json: set cleanUrls=false for serve. Validation: (1) /components/tabs.html now returns 200 with filename=tabs.html and fragment markup, (2) /components/file-upload.html returns fragment markup, (3) /index.html returns app shell normally. This prevents HTMX from injecting index shell into component containers.
close_reason: Resolved by disabling cleanUrls so HTMX component .html routes no longer rewrite to extensionless SPA fallback paths.

comments.author: Your Name
comments.text:
QA reproduction (DarkDog, 2026-02-10):
- With SPA fallback server (npx serve -s ui_v2_exp -l 4174):
  - GET /components/tabs.html -> 301 Location: /components/tabs
  - GET /components/tabs -> 200 serving index.html (full app shell), not fragment
- With non-SPA static server (python3 -m http.server):
  - GET /ui_v2_exp/components/tabs.html -> 200 fragment markup (tabs only)
Conclusion: bug reproduced; root cause is SPA fallback/routing behavior for fragment endpoints.
Suggested owner fix: ensure components/*.html bypasses SPA fallback or serve with static server that preserves .html fragment paths.
Status: NOT RESOLVED (implementation needed in ui_v2_exp owner scope).

title: Add Batch tab workflow in ui_v2_exp
description: Implement batch scenario authoring, validation, submit flow, and polling integration with backend batch APIs.
close_reason: Batch tab workflow implemented in ui_v2_exp with tests and verification

title: Configuration JSON upload fails with missing handler runtime error
description:
Environment/context:
- Project: ui_v2_exp
- URL: http://127.0.0.1:4174/index.html (local static serve)
- Browser validation run on 2026-02-10
- Test data path: /home/omv/general/planner/test_data/06_leg_4+4_start_all_in_jan/priority_config.json

Repro steps:
1. Open ui_v2_exp in browser.
2. Navigate to Configuration tab.
3. In JSON upload area, upload priority_config.json (via file picker/drop).
4. Observe UI uploaded-files list and console errors.

Expected result:
- Uploaded JSON file appears in Configuration uploaded-files list.
- JSON is parsed/applied to configuration state with no runtime errors.

Actual result:
- Uploaded-files list remains empty (0 files shown).
- Runtime errors appear and block handler execution:
  - "Failed to execute 'insertBefore' on 'Node': Unexpected identifier 'handleJsonFileUpload'"
  - "handleJsonFileUpload is not defined"

Severity / priority recommendation:
- Severity: High (blocks configuration import core flow).
- Recommended Beads priority: P1.

Verification notes:
- CSV upload in Input Data works with files:
  data_equipment.csv, data_fte.csv, data_legs.csv, data_test.csv, data_test_duts.csv
- localStorage.parsedCsvData contains all uploaded CSV filenames, confirming CSV path is healthy while JSON path is broken.
close_reason: Fixed: Updated loadJsonConfiguration() method to match actual config structure. Changed field names from legacy format (leg_deadlines, deadline_penalty_per_day) to current format (deadlines, penaltySettings, proximityRules). Changed sectionStates to sectionEnabled.

title: ui_v2_exp data editor row operations do not persist to file store
description: In ui_v2_exp/components/data-editor.html row add/remove actions update local table state but do not persist back into fileStore, causing edits to be lost. Parity gap vs ui/config_editor.html inline mutation behavior.
close_reason: Made data editor add/remove row operations persist to fileStore/localStorage immediately

title: Tabs are not keyboard/screen-reader accessible
description:
Environment/context:
- ui_v2_exp served locally via python3 -m http.server on http://127.0.0.1:4174/index.html
- Browser MCP desktop run (Tue Feb 10 2026)

Repro steps:
1. Open ui_v2_exp index page.
2. Press Tab repeatedly from top of page to navigate interactive controls.
3. Attempt to focus and activate tab labels (Input Data, Edit Data, Configuration, Solver, Visualizer, Output Data) using keyboard (Enter/Space).
4. Inspect accessibility tree snapshot.

Expected result:
- Each tab is a focusable control (button/tab role) with a clear accessible name and active state.
- Keyboard users can move focus to each tab and activate it with Enter/Space.
- Accessibility tree exposes each tab as an individual interactive element.

Actual result:
- Tab labels are rendered as plain text in the accessibility snapshot, not individual interactive controls.
- Keyboard Tab navigation does not expose/focus tab controls; they cannot be activated via keyboard.
- Tabs are implemented as non-semantic <div> elements with click handlers only.

Severity / priority recommendation:
- Severity: High (blocks keyboard-only users from primary app navigation; accessibility failure on core flow).
- Recommended Beads priority: P1.
close_reason: Fixed: Converted tab <div> elements to accessible <button> elements with proper ARIA attributes. Tabs now have role=tab, aria-selected, aria-controls, and keyboard support (Enter/Space). Tab panels have role=tabpanel with aria-labelledby. Focus styles already present in CSS.

title: Persist visualization template selection in ui_v2_exp
description: Selected visualization template in ui_v2_exp/components/visualizer.html is not persisted across reloads. Add localStorage-backed persistence for template preference to match legacy UX expectations.
close_reason: Verified visualization template selection already persists via localStorage and restore flow

title: ui_v2_exp section enable toggles do not update solverConfig
description:
**Context:** Another agent ran a verification pass on ui_v2_exp (2026-02-11).

**Repro Steps (high-level):**
1) Open Configuration tab.
2) Toggle a section enable/disable checkbox.
3) Observe state changes.

**Expected Behavior:**
- Toggling a section checkbox should:
  - Update 
  - Trigger  / recompute 
  - Set  to 

**Actual Behavior (FAIL):**
- Toggling section checkbox updates .
-  remains unchanged.
-  stays .
- Output config is not recomputed.

**Impact:**
- Section enable/disable does not fully propagate to solver output settings.
- Matches original issue description for planner-y7u (closed 2026-02-10).

**References:**
- Original issue: planner-y7u (closed 2026-02-10)
- Related: planner-30d (JSON upload runtime errors)
notes: Re-verified via playwright-cli on 2026-02-11: could not reproduce in this run. Toggling Leg Deadlines changed sectionEnabled.deadlinesEnabled to false and removed leg_deadlines from priority_config_settings output.
close_reason: Fixed: Wrapped section enable checkbox change handlers with $nextTick() to resolve race condition between x-model and x-on:change events

No run-session lifecycle router/contracts (create/upload/solve/status/results) detected in baseline scan. Conclusion: planner-294 remains not resolved from QA perspective until new run-session endpoints and response models are implemented and wired.

comments.author: Your Name
comments.text:
QA baseline (DarkDog, 2026-02-10):
- Current parser behavior in  accepts  and  with single-digit week (), but rejects legacy/canonical variants needed by issue scope:
  -  -> ValueError (missing -W split)
  -  -> ValueError (int conversion)
- Validation path () also expects strict  + integer week token.
- Backend ingest () currently saves CSV payloads directly and does not normalize week fields before planner_v4 load.
Conclusion: planner-2eb remains NOT RESOLVED; canonicalization at ingest boundary is still required.
Suggested acceptance checks after implementation:
1) Input rows with , , ,  accepted per policy and normalized to canonical .
2) Persisted snapshots/settings expose canonical value only.
3) Re-run parser smoke to confirm no ValueError on accepted legacy forms.

comments.author: Your Name
comments.text: QA correction note (DarkDog): prior comment formatting was mangled by shell substitution. Correct baseline evidence follows. parse_iso_week accepts 2025-W02 and 2025-W2, but rejects 2025-02 (ValueError split) and 2025-W02.0 (ValueError int conversion). validate_iso_week_format also requires -W plus integer week token. backend/src/services/solver_service.py currently writes incoming CSV payloads directly with no week normalization step before planner_v4 load. Conclusion: planner-2eb remains not resolved; ingest-boundary canonicalization still required.

title: ui_v2_exp data editor state inconsistency after CSV upload
description: Context: deterministic Playwright retest on 2026-02-11 against http://localhost:4174/index.html. Repro: upload five CSV fixtures from /home/omv/general/planner/test_data/06_leg_4+4_start_all_in_jan, open Edit Data, select data_equipment.csv, run Add Row and Remove Selected Row flows, then reload and reselect the file. Expected: one option per uploaded file; stable file metadata tracking; Add Row increments by one; Remove Selected Row enables after row selection and persists to parsed data/local storage. Actual: uploadedFiles localStorage key is missing while parsedCsvData is populated; file selector options are duplicated many times; one Add Row action increases rows from 8 to 11 (+3); Remove Selected Row remains disabled after row click; inflated row count persists after reload. Severity: medium-high because edit workflow becomes inconsistent and unreliable.
notes: Automated smoke script run: scripts/ui_v2_exp_playwright_smoke.sh (report ui_v2_exp/qa_reports/playwright_smoke_20260211_122017.md). Reconfirmed uploadedFiles localStorage missing, duplicated file selector options, Add Row single click inflates data_equipment rows from 8 to 11, Remove Selected Row disabled, and inflated count persists after reload.
close_reason: Fixed CSV upload state inconsistency. Modified fileStore.js to reconstruct uploadedFiles from parsedCsvData keys instead of deserializing from localStorage (File objects cannot be serialized). All 8 tests pass in fileStore.test.js.

title: Run sessions and batch scenario pipeline
description: Implement backend-owned run sessions, canonical input normalization, and multi-scenario batch execution with reproducible artifact packaging for ui_v2_exp + backend + planner_v4.
notes: Coordination update (LilacCove, 2026-02-10): Execution order agreed for epic delivery: Phase 1 backend foundation (planner-294 + planner-2eb), Phase 2 contract/storage (planner-33j + planner-37w + planner-14q), Phase 3 batch core (planner-2py + planner-2ec + planner-l7s), Phase 4 UX/artifacts (planner-1d8 + planner-2ml + planner-164), Phase 5 enhancements (planner-rbh). Deconflict rule: backend tickets stay with backend owner, planner_v4 tickets with planner owner, ui_v2_exp tickets with UI owner. Epic remains IN_PROGRESS until cross-stack acceptance criteria and end-to-end batch run evidence are complete.

Test baseline: pytest backend/tests/test_run_sessions.py currently fails at collection in this environment due missing dependency fastapi (ModuleNotFoundError), so functional API verification is blocked here. Conclusion: planner-2lx not resolved; backend piece appears in progress, but cross-stack epic acceptance (ui + planner_v4 integration + runnable test env) remains incomplete.

title: Configuration tab emits runtime errors after JSON upload
description:
Environment/context:
- ui_v2_exp at http://127.0.0.1:4174/index.html
- Test data: /home/omv/general/planner/test_data/06_leg_4+4_start_all_in_jan/priority_config.json
- Browser validation run: 2026-02-10

Repro steps:
1. Open ui_v2_exp.
2. Go to Configuration tab.
3. Upload priority_config.json.
4. Observe browser console/runtime logs.

Expected result:
- JSON uploads cleanly with no runtime exceptions.

Actual result:
- Upload appears to complete, but runtime errors are emitted:
  - "Global error: null" (repeated)
  - "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'after')"
  - "init is not defined"

Severity / priority recommendation:
- Severity: Medium (flow works but runtime exceptions indicate unstable behavior and potential hidden regressions).
- Recommended Beads priority: P2.

Verification notes:
- Input Data CSV upload still passes for all five CSV test files.
- This issue is scoped to post-upload runtime stability in Configuration flow.
notes: Fresh QA pass prioritized user complaint. Tabs currently appear low-prominence/non-interactive in Browser MCP snapshot (tracked separately as planner-3j0). planner-30d remains specifically about post-JSON-upload runtime errors and still needs live upload recheck once Browser MCP interaction path is stable.
close_reason: Fixed post-JSON-upload runtime errors by removing invalid x-init usage and adding HTMX/error null guards

title: Implement deterministic run artifact folder contract
description: Persist run artifacts under runs/<timestamp>_<run_name>/<run_id>/ with input_original, input_effective, output, plots, and settings_used.json.
notes: Progress update (LilacCove, 2026-02-10): scoped artifact contract details for implementation handoff. Required folder shape per run: runs/<timestamp>_<run_name>/<run_id>/{input_original,input_effective,output,plots}/ plus settings_used.json. Acceptance checks: (1) deterministic folder naming, (2) always write run directory for success/failure, (3) write metadata manifest with status and timestamps, (4) retain parity with current solver outputs under output/. Dependency alignment: planner-294 API lifecycle should emit stable run_id + run_name before storage write step.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic run artifact folder contract with tests

comments.author: Your Name
comments.text: QA baseline (DarkDog, 2026-02-10): Deterministic artifact contract is not yet satisfied in current baseline. backend/src/services/solver_service.py executes runs in temporary workspaces and cleans them up, with outputs returned from memory paths; no persistent contract path like runs/<timestamp>_<run_name>/<run_id>/ with input_original, input_effective, output, plots, and settings_used.json was found in QA scan. API route layer (including runs_batch) does not enforce artifact layout. Existing backend tests (test_run_sessions.py, test_batch_jobs.py) validate endpoint lifecycles but do not assert deterministic artifact directory structure. Conclusion: planner-33j remains unresolved from QA baseline until deterministic artifact contract is implemented and verified.

title: Add input snapshot integrity for reproducible runs

close_reason: Snapshot integrity contract already satisfied by deterministic artifact implementation

title: ui_v2_exp tab navigation emits uploadedFiles undefined and duplicate-key console errors
description:
Context: Browser MCP console run after server restart on port 4174.

Repro:
1) Open http://127.0.0.1:4174/index.html.
2) Navigate across tabs including Edit Data and Visualizer.
3) Observe browser console.

Expected:
Tab switches initialize components without Alpine runtime warnings/errors.

Actual:
Console repeatedly emits:
- Alpine Expression Error: uploadedFiles is not defined
- Alpine Warning: Duplicate key on x-for
- Alpine Expression Error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading after)
- Global error: null

Evidence:
Console log includes Data editor initialization followed by repeated uploadedFiles/duplicate-key errors and uncaught Alpine exceptions.

Severity: medium (P2), causes noisy runtime state and unstable tab behavior.
close_reason: Fixed tab navigation runtime errors by adding uploadedFiles getter and deterministic x-for keys with regression tests

title: ui_v2_exp tabs are rendered as non-interactive text in Browser MCP session
description:
Context: Browser MCP check at http://127.0.0.1:4174/index.html (2026-02-10).

Repro:
1) Open page.
2) Observe top navigation area.

Expected:
Tab bar exposes clear clickable/focusable tab controls with active state.

Actual:
Labels appear as plain muted text in accessibility snapshot, with no interactive tab nodes discoverable. User reports page looks wrong/missing tabs.

Evidence:
- Browser snapshot lists tab labels as static text only.
- Screenshot shows very low-prominence tab row and no obvious affordance.

Severity: medium user-visible UX defect.
notes: Browser MCP live run captured user concern: page renders with very low-contrast tab labels that are exposed as plain text in snapshot, making tabs appear missing/non-interactive. Console initially clean on load; issue is primarily visible/interaction affordance and tab semantics.
close_reason: Fixed tab interactivity/keyboard behavior in ui_v2_exp tabs and app navigation handler

title: ui_v2_exp ignores hash-based initial tab selection
description:
Context: Re-verification on 2026-02-11 using static server (python3 -m http.server 4174).

Repro:
1) Open http://localhost:4174/index.html#visualizer
2) Observe selected tab and visible content immediately after load.
3) Repeat with #edit_data.

Expected:
- App should initialize active tab from URL hash when hash is a valid tab id (e.g., visualizer, edit_data).

Actual:
- Input Data remains selected and input upload prompt remains visible; hash value is ignored.

Evidence:
- Browser snapshots at #visualizer and #edit_data both show tablist with Input Data selected and upload prompt text.

Severity: medium (deep-linking and state restore behavior broken; may indicate tab activation/init path regression).

Note: Browser MCP click actions were intermittently timing out in this session, so this issue is scoped specifically to deterministic hash-load behavior, not click automation reliability.
close_reason: Fixed hash-based initial tab selection and hash/tab synchronization with regression tests
